name: Bashir ibn Hazlam
title: Messenger to Medina
shortDescription: Imam Sajjad's (AS) envoy who entered Medina before the captives' caravan and announced the Imam's martyrdom with a heartbreaking poem.
fullStory:
Bashir ibn Hazlam was a lover of the Ahl al-Bayt (AS) who accompanied the caravan of Karbala captives on their return from Damascus to Medina. He was a skilled poet.

When the caravan neared Medina, Imam Sajjad (AS) ordered a halt and told Bashir: 'O Bashir! Your father was a poet, do you also compose poetry? Go to Medina and announce the martyrdom of Aba Abdullah and our arrival.'

Bashir mounted his horse and swiftly entered Medina. He went near the Prophet's Mosque and, while weeping, loudly recited a historic poem: 'O people of Yathrib (Medina)! There is no longer a place for you to stay here, for Hussain has been killed and my tears are flowing for him...'

Upon hearing Bashir's voice, all the men and women of Medina rushed out of their houses crying and wailing. Medina turned into a house of mourning, and the people hurried outside the city to welcome the caravan of Karbala survivors.
quoteArabic: يا أَهْلَ يَثْرِبَ لا مُقامَ لَكُمْ بِها... قُتِلَ الحُسَيْنُ فَأَدْمُعِي مِدْرارُ...
quote: (His famous poem:) O people of Yathrib! There is no stay for you here, Hussain has been killed and my tears pour down... His body is drenched in blood and his head is atop a spear.
